A&E for Solihull petition

  • Tweet

As the Member of Parliament for Meriden & Solihull East, my constituents' access to healthcare services is of the utmost importance to me. We have several wonderful healthcare facilities across Solihull borough but we must continue to improve our healthcare network to better serve the local community. Our next step is to restore Accident and Emergency (A&E) services to the Solihull borough. At the moment, residents of the Solihull borough have to travel to different areas to access an A&E department, with many travelling to Bordesley Green to attend Heartlands Hospital. For many people within our community, travelling to Bordesley Green is impractical and arduous, making accessing A&E services difficult. 

Our community deserves the best healthcare facilities available, and restoring an A&E department to the Solihull borough is paramount to developing our access to emergency medical services. I am campaigning on this issue by talking to the Health Secretary and the Head of the NHS Birmingham and Solihull Integrated Care Board to bring this issue to their attention and ask them how we can best bring A&E services to the Solihull borough. You can support this campaign by signing my petition for this initiative. Together, we can secure an A&E department for the Solihull borough and make sure that everyone has convenient access to this vital emergency medical service.

News

Saqib relaunches his ‘A&E for Solihull’ Petition

Wednesday, 27 November, 2024
Saqib has relaunched his ‘A&E for Solihull’ petition jointly with Dr Neil Shastri-Hurst MP, Member of Parliament for Solihull West and Shirley on Friday 22nd November.

Saqib calls for A&E services to be delivered to the Solihull borough

Tuesday, 19 November, 2024
Saqib has called for greater healthcare infrastructure and the restoration of A&E services to the Solihull Borough during the Department of Health and Social Care's oral parliamentary questions. He noted that there are already 220,000 people residing in the Solihull borough and that the gov

Saqib Bhatti MP and Dr Neil Shastri-Hurst MP jointly call on the Health Secretary to improve healthcare provision within the Solihull borough

Tuesday, 19 November, 2024
Saqib and Dr Neil Shastri-Hurst, as MPs for the Solihull borough, have jointly written to the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care to ask for his support in securing more provision for healthcare within the Solihull borough.

Saqib appointed as a Shadow Minister in the Department of Science, Innovation and Technology and the Department of Health and Social Care

Monday, 29 July, 2024
Saqib has been appointed as a Shadow Minister of State in the Department of Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) and a Shadow Minister in the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) in charge of primary care and dentistry.

Saqib Bhatti Conservative candidate for Meriden & Solihull East
